ordering: entries are sorted by series_order ascending, then prompt_family_id ascending

entries.rationale: Capability vocabulary already appears in pack, control, and compatibility surfaces, but it is not yet frozen as one semantic framework.
entries.next_action_implication: Normalize and pin existing capability surfaces during Λ instead of inventing a parallel model.

entries.rationale: Cross-domain bridge work is real and needed, but the field, schema, and pack split-ownership zones make final bridge semantics unsafe to freeze silently.
entries.next_action_implication: Map bridge candidates first, then route any disputed ownership through explicit review.

entries.rationale: The repo already has domain packs, law packs, experience packs, and schema-backed contract surfaces strong enough to support a formal template.
entries.next_action_implication: Author the template as a consolidation of existing pack and schema law surfaces.

entries.rationale: The repo already contains inference, proof, planning, and formalization-adjacent domain material, but not one frozen semantic chain.
entries.next_action_implication: Freeze the chain from live semantic and proof surfaces rather than from abstract theory alone.

entries.rationale: Experience packs and profile surfaces already embody parts of the acceptance and refusal space, but the map itself is not formalized.
entries.next_action_implication: Formalize player-facing acceptance boundaries from existing experience and law surfaces.

entries.rationale: Truth/perceived/render separation already exists constitutionally and in live control/view and presentation surfaces, but the ladder is not explicitly frozen.
entries.next_action_implication: Freeze the ladder by extending the existing observer, perception, and render surfaces.

entries.rationale: The repo already has broad reality, world, universe, material, logic, and signal roots that make a blank-slate framework unnecessary.
entries.next_action_implication: Formalize the framework directly from the live semantic roots.

entries.rationale: The baseline governance problem is already materially solved by AGENTS, canon, glossary, and the P-0 planning authority stack.
entries.next_action_implication: Do not create a new governance baseline; preserve and extend the one that already exists.

entries.family_name: Agent mirrors and vendor-neutral instruction surfaces
entries.rationale: The repo already contains mirrored advisory instruction layers and XStack-related guidance, but they are fragmented rather than harmonized.
entries.next_action_implication: Merge and normalize the existing mirror surfaces immediately after initial Λ framing.

entries.rationale: Safety, refusal, manual review, and boundary rules already exist across AGENTS, governance docs, ControlX, and XStack surfaces.
entries.next_action_implication: Preserve the existing safety baseline and only consolidate wording and routing.

entries.rationale: Wrapper infrastructure already exists, but canonical wrapper contracts should inherit stable capability and task semantics from Λ first.
entries.next_action_implication: Delay wrapper freezing until after Λ and the governed task vocabulary are in place.

entries.rationale: Natural-language binding is high leverage, but it should not freeze before Λ stabilizes capability surfaces, semantic bridges, and acceptance boundaries.
entries.next_action_implication: Define the bridge only after Λ core vocabulary lands.

entries.rationale: The XStack substrate is strong, but a canonical task catalog should not lock task classes before Λ freezes the semantic and capability vocabulary they target.
entries.next_action_implication: Reuse the live XStack tooling, but delay the final catalog until after Λ.

entries.rationale: The repo already acknowledges tool and asset-pipeline surfaces, but there is not yet one frozen runtime-facing asset pipeline boundary.
entries.next_action_implication: Map the existing tooling and pipeline surfaces before any deeper render or live-ops work.

entries.rationale: The runtime substrate is real, but component ownership cannot be frozen safely before Λ resolves semantic and pack-law grounding.
entries.next_action_implication: Derive the component model from the live runtime substrate only after Λ core semantics are pinned.

entries.rationale: The repo has only precursor references for authority handoff and shard behavior; freezing distributed authority now would outrun both runtime and semantic grounding.
entries.next_action_implication: Keep this at design-gate level only until lifecycle, snapshot, proof, and Λ semantics are frozen.

entries.rationale: Event-log, replay, checkpoint, and drift surfaces already exist, but there is not one frozen runtime event-log contract yet.
entries.next_action_implication: Extend the existing event and replay surfaces rather than introducing a new logging universe.

entries.rationale: Render backends and client renderers exist, but the explicit framegraph and render-device foundations called for in the blueprint are not yet present.
entries.next_action_implication: Do not execute render replacement work until the missing abstractions are mapped and introduced deliberately.

entries.rationale: Hotswap and replacement work would freeze the wrong boundaries before lifecycle, snapshot, component, and semantic contracts are stable.
entries.next_action_implication: Treat hotswap only as a late boundary-design concern, not an early runtime task.

entries.rationale: No explicit lifecycle-manager surface is present in the live repo, and the blueprint treats it as a required new foundation.
entries.next_action_implication: Map insertion points first; do not imply that lifecycle control already exists as a frozen subsystem.

entries.rationale: The repo does not expose an explicit module-loader foundation yet, and the blueprint stop conditions still treat loader insertion points as unresolved.
entries.next_action_implication: Leave this blocked until runtime boundaries are mapped against the live substrate.

entries.rationale: Compat and migration surfaces are strong, but final coexistence rules depend on the unresolved schema and pack ownership splits.
entries.next_action_implication: Keep coexistence planning tied to quarantine resolution rather than freezing it early.

entries.rationale: The engine/game spine and app/process/runtime substrate already embody a kernel direction, so a greenfield kernel prompt would be wasteful.
entries.next_action_implication: Extract the model from the live spine instead of inventing a new kernel story.

entries.rationale: Runtime, compatibility, control, persistence, and observability services already exist in distributed form across live roots.
entries.next_action_implication: Formalize service boundaries from existing surfaces instead of creating a fresh service layer.

entries.rationale: Sandbox tests and sandbox-like product surfaces exist, but a canonical runtime sandbox model is not yet frozen.
entries.next_action_implication: Extend the existing sandbox and refusal surfaces instead of assuming the repo has no sandbox model.

entries.rationale: Snapshot, checkpoint, and replay artifacts exist, but there is no unified snapshot service or handoff boundary yet.
entries.next_action_implication: Do not treat schemas and checkpoint code as proof that the service boundary already exists.

entries.rationale: Export, import, migration, checkpoint, and replay surfaces already exist, but final state boundaries should inherit Λ semantics instead of guessing what counts as lawful externalized state.
entries.next_action_implication: Pin state classes only after Λ clarifies domain and truth ownership boundaries.

entries.rationale: Archive support, archive policy code, and archive policy registries already exist, but the final publication constitution is not frozen.
entries.next_action_implication: Consolidate existing archive and mirror rules rather than inventing them from scratch.

entries.rationale: The repo already has a governed build and structure spine strong enough to support a dedicated build-graph lock artifact.
entries.next_action_implication: Freeze the lock from existing build and architecture surfaces.

entries.rationale: The preset, toolchain, and CI matrix is already rich enough that reproducibility work is a normalization step, not a discovery step.
entries.next_action_implication: Turn the existing matrix into a governed reproducibility artifact.

entries.rationale: Rollback selection, yanked handling, and downgrade-related release behavior already exist, but the policy is not yet frozen under one explicit constitution.
entries.next_action_implication: Consolidate downgrade and rollback policy from existing release machinery.

entries.rationale: Capability and trust surfaces already exist across pack metadata, schema law, and secure tooling, but they are not yet one consolidated release policy layer.
entries.next_action_implication: Unify the existing capability and trust policy surfaces instead of inventing new ones.

entries.rationale: The repo already contains parity tests, governed command wrappers, and UI binding surfaces, but not one frozen parity doctrine.
entries.next_action_implication: Codify parity around the existing test and tool surfaces.

entries.rationale: Install/update transaction log machinery already exists, but the broader operator-transaction contract is not yet generalized.
entries.next_action_implication: Extend the live transaction-log substrate into a broader operator contract instead of creating one from zero.

entries.rationale: The preset matrix is already present and mapped, so consolidation is ready to proceed once upstream sequencing is respected.
entries.next_action_implication: Normalize the existing preset families rather than rebuilding them.

entries.rationale: Channel manifests, distribution tooling, and product distribution names already exist, but the final publication model is not frozen under one doctrine.
entries.next_action_implication: Formalize publication models as a consolidation of existing channels and distribution surfaces.

entries.family_name: Release contract profile and related artifacts
entries.rationale: The exact release contract profile artifacts do not exist under those names yet, but the repo already has build-ID, artifact-identity, release-channel, and policy substrate strong enough to define them.
entries.next_action_implication: Author the missing release contract profile artifacts as consolidation over existing identity and release surfaces.

entries.rationale: Release policy, update indices, yanked selection, and trust checks already exist, but the policy is still distributed across code, manifests, and audits.
entries.next_action_implication: Consolidate the existing release index logic into a clearer constitution.

entries.rationale: The repo already has release tooling, operator tooling, and XStack control surfaces, but not one final governed ops model.
entries.next_action_implication: Freeze release ops by consolidating existing tools and policies.

entries.rationale: Release, distribution, compatibility, and secure tooling already form a real pipeline substrate, but the pipeline is not yet frozen as one governed flow.
entries.next_action_implication: Define the pipeline as a consolidation of the existing tooling and policy stack.

entries.rationale: The repo already distinguishes schema, protocol, pack, feature-epoch, product, and build identity truths; the missing work is to freeze them into one explicit constitution.
entries.next_action_implication: Write the constitution as a consolidation of existing explicit identity surfaces, not as a fresh invention.

entries.rationale: Cluster-of-clusters, live schema evolution by default, and restartless core replacement remain explicitly unrealistic or speculative in the current blueprint and repo state.
entries.next_action_implication: Keep these out of near-term execution planning.

entries.rationale: Migration, checkpoint, and rollback precursors exist, but online save migration still depends on snapshot-service, lifecycle, and state-externalization foundations that are not frozen.
entries.next_action_implication: Do not schedule live save migration before Φ and Υ foundations land.

entries.rationale: Save and snapshot primitives exist, but non-blocking capture still depends on lifecycle control and explicit snapshot-service boundaries.
entries.next_action_implication: Leave this blocked until the missing Φ foundations are real.

entries.rationale: The blueprint still classifies live module reload as requiring new foundation, and the repo still lacks the module loader and state-export boundaries it depends on.
entries.next_action_implication: Keep this behind Φ module-loader and state-externalization work.

entries.rationale: The repo has useful runtime and rollback precursors, but hot-swappable subsystem work would still outrun lifecycle, state-export, render, and cutover foundations.
entries.next_action_implication: Treat replaceability as a late-stage governed outcome, not a near-term implementation target.

entries.rationale: Rollback, yanked-release, and transaction-log machinery already exist, but canary and live rollout orchestration are not yet fully frozen.
entries.next_action_implication: Treat early Ζ rollout work as an extension of existing rollback policy, not a blank-slate live-ops project.

entries.rationale: Supervisor and shell surfaces exist, but runtime-safe service restart semantics would still be guessed without a frozen lifecycle manager and cutover policy.
entries.next_action_implication: Keep restart work downstream of explicit Φ lifecycle and Υ release control work.

entries.family_name: Shard relocation and distributed live ops
entries.rationale: The repo has shard and replay precursors, but the blueprint still treats distributed relocation as unrealistic without deterministic replication and lawful authority handoff.
entries.next_action_implication: Do not plan this in detail yet; keep it behind Φ and Υ completion plus explicit human approval.

entries.family_name: Trust-root rotation and live trust changes
entries.rationale: Trust verification tooling is strong, but live trust rotation still depends on controlled distribution, operator transaction logging, and rollback choreography that are not yet frozen.
entries.next_action_implication: Keep live trust changes behind later Υ control-plane work.
